title: pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. rehderi
intro: Pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. rehderi, also known as Rehder's Douglas-fir, is a subspecies of the Douglas fir tree native to China. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. rehderi include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in traditional Chinese medicine, often in the form of teas, infusions, or decoctions, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
